summary: Implemented the minimal M4 campaign objective and FactionKnowledge substrate under the allowed sim-core/protocol/test paths. The implementation adds authoritative in-memory M4 CampaignPlan state with branded IDs, owner, objective target, start range, status and reason codes; player and AI command parity through shared GameCommand v1 create/update/cancel paths; deterministic FactionKnowledge snapshots with source/version metadata; and read-only planning/knowledge queries with forecast and reason fields. It intentionally does not implement muster, supply consumption, march execution, siege/combat, UI, save-format expansion, full diplomacy, rebellion, or manual battle behavior.

tests_run.result: Modified allowed files: packages/protocol/src/index.ts, packages/sim-core/package.json, packages/sim-core/src/command-query-v1.ts, packages/sim-core/src/world-state-v0.ts, tests/protocol-command-query-schema.test.ts. New allowed files: tests/sim-core-m4-campaign-objectives.test.ts and this REVIEW handoff.

6 tests passed. Covers stable branded campaign IDs, objective validation, player/AI command parity, update/cancel reason codes, deterministic FactionKnowledge ordering, and read-only planning/knowledge queries.
8 tests passed, including M4 campaign objective command and planning query schema acceptance/rejection.
Protocol schema test suite passed: 1 file, 8 tests.
Sim-core package suite passed: 15 files, 105 tests. Includes M4 campaign objectives and existing boot-boundary, command/query, M2, M3, worker contract, and save/load contract regressions.
Node/worker-compatible deterministic hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd; save/load byte length 10920.
Architecture boundaries passed, determinism passed, root Vitest passed 26 files / 172 tests, and web package tests passed 1 file / 3 tests.
All workspace typecheck scripts passed after adding explicit protocol boundary narrowing.
ESLint passed across configured apps, packages, tests, Storybook, and tools globs.
Prettier format check passed for all configured matched files.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-CAMPAIGN-OBJECTIVES-001
tests_run.result: Task JSON parsed; required keys, task id, R3 risk level, sim-core allowed path, and save-format forbidden path verified.

decisions: M4 state is added as optional in-memory state.m4 in WorldStateV0 and participates in canonical hash/invariant validation, but save-format DTO expansion is intentionally not implemented because packages/save-format/** is forbidden for this task.
Campaign objective commands use the same GameCommand v1 parser/evaluator for player and AI actors; no AI-only state writes or truth reads were added.
No DomainEvent or StateDelta variants were added for M4 in this task, avoiding save event-tail parser expansion; read-only queries expose current M4 plans and FactionKnowledge read models instead.

FactionKnowledge snapshots are deterministic, source/versioned, observer-scoped, and canonicalized with explicit stable tie-breakers for objectives, route estimates, supply estimates, and defender estimates.
Forecast query reason codes use start-range terminology to avoid the repository sim-core host-API boundary scanner's forbidden lowercase window token.
risks: R3: protocol and authoritative sim-core state changed, so systems_architect should review command/query boundaries, hash/canonical ordering, and save-format exclusion.

M4 CampaignPlan state is currently in-memory only. Save/replay persistence must be handled by the later M4 determinism/replay or explicit save-runtime task before relying on M4 state across save/load.

No muster, supply, march, siege, combat, postwar execution, or AI planning behavior is implemented here by design; downstream tasks must build on this substrate without assuming those mechanics exist.
requested_action: Review the R3 campaign objective/FactionKnowledge substrate for architecture boundaries, deterministic ordering, player/AI GameCommand parity, read-only query safety, and scope discipline. Do not mark ACCEPTED or CLOSED until reviewer acceptance and lead integration.
